@@ -94,13 +94,21 @@ Current main now wires both tool-call hooks, passes structured exec results thro
 ## Close Comment
 
 Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
 Current main now wires both tool-call hooks, passes structured exec results through `after_tool_call`, and exposes the runtime primitives needed for a plugin to enqueue a follow-up retry turn. That matches the issue’s preferred hook-based solution well enough to close as implemented on main.
+
 What I checked:
-- **`before_tool_call` is now in the live tool path:** The tool-definition adapter calls `runBeforeToolCallHook(...)` before execution and honors blocked/adjusted params.
+
+- **`before_tool_call` is now in the live tool path:** The tool-definition adapter calls `runBeforeToolCallHook(...)` before execution and honors blocked/adjusted params. ([`src/agents/pi-tool-definition-adapter.ts:231`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa/src/agents/pi-tool-definition-adapter.ts#L231), [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa))
+- **`after_tool_call` is now fired on tool completion:** `handleToolExecutionEnd(...)` invokes `runAfterToolCall(...)` with `toolName`, adjusted `params`, `result`, `error`, `durationMs`, and tool context. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:1122`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts#L1122), [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa))
+- **The hook contract carries structured tool results:** `PluginHookAfterToolCallEvent` includes `result`, `error`, and `durationMs`, and result sanitization only truncates content while preserving other fields like `details`. ([`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:339`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa/src/plugins/hook-types.ts#L339), [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa))
+- **Exec results already expose `exitCode` structurally:** `buildExecForegroundResult(...)` returns tool results with `details.exitCode` for both failed and completed foreground exec outcomes. ([`src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts:71`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa/src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts#L71), [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa))
+- **Plugins already have the retry primitives this issue proposed:** Plugin runtime exposes `enqueueSystemEvent` and `requestHeartbeatNow`, and the SDK runtime docs show the same `api.runtime.system` surface for plugins. ([`src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts:74`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa/src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts#L74), [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa))
+- **Hook wiring is covered by dedicated tests:** The e2e hook test asserts that `handleToolExecutionEnd(...)` calls `runAfterToolCall(...)` exactly once with the expected payload. ([`src/plugins/wired-hooks-after-tool-call.e2e.test.ts:115`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa/src/plugins/wired-hooks-after-tool-call.e2e.test.ts#L115), [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa))
+
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
-Review notes: reviewed against [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa); fix evidence: commit [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa).
 
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa). Fix evidence: commit [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa).
+Review notes: reviewed against [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa); fix evidence: commit [d4d307e07a86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d4d307e07a863823bbb401fdaeb6c00ebeaf4dfa).

@@ -90,13 +90,21 @@ Current `main` no longer requires hardcoded OpenRouter catalog entries for new r
 ## Close Comment
 
 Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
 Current `main` no longer requires hardcoded OpenRouter catalog entries for new refs. The OpenRouter plugin now resolves arbitrary model IDs dynamically, the embedded runner calls that path before surfacing `Unknown model`, and OpenClaw now ships an OpenRouter free-model scan workflow.
+
 What I checked:
-- **OpenRouter plugin resolves arbitrary model IDs:** The bundled OpenRouter provider builds runtime models from the requested `ctx.modelId` and registers both `resolveDynamicModel` and `prepareDynamicModel`, so new OpenRouter slugs do not need a bundled catalog row first.
+
+- **OpenRouter plugin resolves arbitrary model IDs:** The bundled OpenRouter provider builds runtime models from the requested `ctx.modelId` and registers both `resolveDynamicModel` and `prepareDynamicModel`, so new OpenRouter slugs do not need a bundled catalog row first. ([`extensions/openrouter/index.ts:49`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/dfe75db09d2d85e23f7d25d7afcbcac4bb30e16e/extensions/openrouter/index.ts#L49), [dfe75db09d2d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/dfe75db09d2d85e23f7d25d7afcbcac4bb30e16e))
+- **Core resolver checks provider dynamic hooks before `Unknown model`:** `resolveModelWithRegistry` tries plugin dynamic resolution before failing, and the async resolver primes `prepareProviderDynamicModel` first, which is the path used for previously unknown OpenRouter refs. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts:673`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts#L673), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **Regression test covers unknown OpenRouter model preload and resolution:** The async model test resolves an unknown OpenRouter model (`google/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview`) after capability preload and expects a successful resolved model instead of `Unknown model`. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts:647`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts#L647), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **OpenRouter free-model scan was added:** `scanOpenRouterModels` now reads OpenRouter's `/models` catalog, filters free entries, and can probe them for tool/image support, which is the intended path for newly published `:free` models. ([`src/agents/model-scan.ts:403`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/dfe75db09d2d85e23f7d25d7afcbcac4bb30e16e/src/agents/model-scan.ts#L403), [dfe75db09d2d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/dfe75db09d2d85e23f7d25d7afcbcac4bb30e16e))
+- **Docs now point users to the free-model scan workflow:** The models docs explicitly document `openclaw models scan` for OpenRouter free models, including probing and selection flow, instead of requiring a bundled catalog update for each new slug. ([`docs/concepts/models.md:231`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/dfe75db09d2d85e23f7d25d7afcbcac4bb30e16e/docs/concepts/models.md#L231), [dfe75db09d2d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/dfe75db09d2d85e23f7d25d7afcbcac4bb30e16e))
+- **Remaining `No endpoints found` failures are treated as upstream model availability:** Failover tests classify OpenRouter `404 No endpoints found for ... :free` responses as `model_not_found`, which matches the later issue comments and indicates the remaining failure mode is upstream endpoint availability rather than missing OpenClaw support. ([`src/agents/failover-error.test.ts:187`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/src/agents/failover-error.test.ts#L187), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
-Review notes: reviewed against [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c); fix evidence: commit [dfe75db09d2d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/dfe75db09d2d85e23f7d25d7afcbcac4bb30e16e).
 
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c). Fix evidence: commit [dfe75db09d2d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/dfe75db09d2d85e23f7d25d7afcbcac4bb30e16e).
+Review notes: reviewed against [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c); fix evidence: commit [dfe75db09d2d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/dfe75db09d2d85e23f7d25d7afcbcac4bb30e16e).

@@ -87,13 +87,21 @@ Current main already treats media-understanding vendors as plugin-owned capabili
 ## Close Comment
 
 Closing this as better suited for [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/)/community plugin work after Codex review.
+
 Current main already treats media-understanding vendors as plugin-owned capability providers, and project scope explicitly says optional capability should usually ship as plugins rather than core. Soniox is absent from main and this request's proposed core-file edits do not match the current architecture.
+
 What I checked:
-- **Vision scope:** Project vision says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, the preferred path is npm/local extension loading, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high.
+
+- **Vision scope:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, the preferred path is npm/local extension loading, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/VISION.md#L54), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Media-understanding registry is plugin-backed:** Current media-understanding registry loads providers from plugin capability providers instead of a hardcoded core registry entry. ([`src/media-understanding/provider-registry.ts:30`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/media-understanding/provider-registry.ts#L30), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Manifest metadata drives provider discovery:** Provider metadata is read from plugin manifests via mediaUnderstandingProviders contracts, so a new vendor fits the plugin manifest path. ([`src/media-understanding/manifest-metadata.ts:6`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/media-understanding/manifest-metadata.ts#L6), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Public SDK supports external media-understanding providers:** The public provider-plugin docs show api.registerMediaUnderstandingProvider(...) as the intended seam for image/audio/video provider plugins. ([`docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md:577`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md#L577), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Existing STT precedent is plugin-shaped:** Deepgram audio transcription is implemented as a plugin entry that registers a media-understanding provider and declares the contract in its plugin manifest. ([`extensions/deepgram/index.ts:5`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/extensions/deepgram/index.ts#L5), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **No Soniox implementation on main:** A repo search across source, docs, extensions, packages, and UI returned no Soniox references on current main. ([cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+
 So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.
-Review notes: reviewed against [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).
 
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

@@ -88,13 +88,20 @@ Current `main` already implements expandable tool-call cards that expose full to
 ## Close Comment
 
 Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
 Current `main` already implements expandable tool-call cards that expose full tool inputs and outputs in the chat UI, so the issue request is satisfied on `main` even though it does not appear to be in a tagged release yet.
+
 What I checked:
-- **Tool cards preserve full tool-call input payloads:** `extractToolCards()` now captures `arguments`/`args`/`input` and serializes them into `inputText`, instead of only relying on the short summary label.
+
+- **Tool cards preserve full tool-call input payloads:** `extractToolCards()` now captures `arguments`/`args`/`input` and serializes them into `inputText`, instead of only relying on the short summary label. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts:140`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc/ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts#L140), [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
+- **Expanded UI shows full tool input and output:** Expanded tool cards render full `Tool input` and `Tool output` blocks, and the side-panel payload includes both sections as well. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts:208`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc/ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts#L208), [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
+- **Clicking tool-call rows now reveals details:** Tool-message rows are rendered as collapsible summaries; clicking the summary toggles an expanded body that renders the detailed tool card content. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:1396`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc/ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts#L1396), [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
+- **Tests cover the reported behavior:** UI tests assert that expanded tool-call rows reveal `Tool input` content and that expanded tool-output rows reveal full JSON/output content. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts:427`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc/ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts#L427), [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
+- **No released tag contains the fix commit yet:** The implementing commit is on `main`, but `git tag --contains 1daa552d` returned no tags, and the latest repo tag is `v2026.4.22`, so this appears unreleased. ([1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
+
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

@@ -90,13 +90,21 @@ Current main treats third-party channels like NoChat as community plugins distri
 ## Close Comment
 
 Closing this as better suited for [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/)/community plugin work after Codex review.
+
 Current main treats third-party channels like NoChat as community plugins distributed through ClawHub/npm, not as bundled core additions. The repo vision, plugin docs, community-plugin docs, CLI install flow, and channel SDK all point to the external-plugin path as the intended scope.
+
 What I checked:
-- **Vision keeps optional capability out of core:** Current main says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, plugin authors should host and maintain their own repo, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high.
+
+- **Vision keeps optional capability out of core:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, plugin authors should host and maintain their own repo, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/VISION.md#L54), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **Plugin guide says not to add plugins to this repo:** The plugin guide explicitly says 'You do not need to add your plugin to the OpenClaw repository' and instead directs authors to publish on ClawHub or npm for `openclaw plugins install`. ([`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:16`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md#L16), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **Community plugin policy points discoverability to ClawHub:** Community-plugin docs call ClawHub the canonical discovery surface and tell authors not to open docs-only PRs for discoverability; publish on ClawHub instead. ([`docs/plugins/community.md:14`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/docs/plugins/community.md#L14), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **External channel plugins are already the documented model:** The same community-plugin docs list third-party channel plugins such as QQbot and WeCom as installable external plugins, showing channels do not need bundling to be supported. ([`docs/plugins/community.md:102`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/docs/plugins/community.md#L102), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **SDK supports optional install-first channel plugins:** Channel-plugin docs direct authors to `createOptionalChannelSetupSurface(...)` when a channel plugin may not be installed yet, so current main explicitly supports non-bundled channel plugins in setup flows. ([`docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md:163`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md#L163), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **User install path is ClawHub-first:** CLI docs route `openclaw plugins install <package>` through ClawHub first, then npm, which is the intended delivery path for community plugins like this one. +- **Vision prefers plugins for optional capability:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says core should stay lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, plugins should be hosted in their own repositories, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/VISION.md#L54), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))

([`VISION.md:99`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/VISION.md#L99), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Current main exposes a plugin-owned context-engine seam:** The architecture docs say context engine plugins own session context orchestration for ingest, assembly, and compaction, selected via `plugins.slots.contextEngine`. The examples show a plugin owning compaction or delegating compaction back to runtime with `delegateCompactionToRuntime(...)`. ([`docs/plugins/architecture.md:1365`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/plugins/architecture.md#L1365), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **SDK and config already support this extension path:** Current main exports `registerContextEngine` and `delegateCompactionToRuntime` from the public plugin SDK, exposes `registerContextEngine(...)` on `OpenClawPluginApi`, and documents `plugins.slots.contextEngine` as the selector for context orchestration behavior. ([`src/plugin-sdk/index.ts:116`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/plugin-sdk/index.ts#L116), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))

-The reason is scope, not merit. Current project direction says optional capability should usually ship as plugins and stay outside core (`VISION.md:54`), and it explicitly calls out agent-hierarchy frameworks and heavy orchestration layers as things OpenClaw is not merging into core by default (`VISION.md:99`). Current main also already provides a dedicated extension path for this exact class of work: context engine plugins own ingest/assembly/compaction and can either own compaction themselves or delegate it back to runtime (`docs/plugins/architecture.md:1365`, `src/plugins/types.ts:2005`, `src/plugin-sdk/index.ts:116`, `src/config/schema.help.ts:1099`). OpenClaw’s docs further say external plugins should be published through ClawHub instead of being added to the repo (`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:16`, `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:125`, `docs/cli/plugins.md:56`).
+So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.

 ## Close Comment
 
 Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
 Current main already implements priority-run interruption through explicit stop handling, interrupt queue mode, and the interrupt-and-steer session API, so the issue request is satisfied.
+
 What I checked:
-- **Inbound interrupt queue mode aborts the active embedded run:** `get-reply-run` checks for `messages.queue.mode === "interrupt"`; when the session lane is busy it clears queued lane work and calls `abortEmbeddedPiRun(...)` before continuing.
+
+- **Inbound interrupt queue mode aborts the active embedded run:** `get-reply-run` checks for `messages.queue.mode === "interrupt"`; when the session lane is busy it clears queued lane work and calls `abortEmbeddedPiRun(...)` before continuing. ([`src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts:597`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts#L597), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **Queue docs describe interrupt semantics:** The queue concept doc now says `interrupt` aborts the active run for that session and runs the newest message; `steer` injects into the active run at the next model boundary. ([`docs/concepts/queue.md:29`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/docs/concepts/queue.md#L29), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **Gateway sessions.steer explicitly interrupts active runs:** `interruptSessionRunIfActive` aborts tracked chat runs, aborts embedded runs, clears queues, and waits for shutdown; `sessions.steer` invokes that path with `interruptIfActive: true`. ([`src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:368`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts#L368), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **STOP-style messages are handled as out-of-band aborts:** Gateway stop detection delegates to abort-text matching; tests cover `/STOP!!!`, `stop please`, `halt`, and multilingual stop phrases, and `chat.send` short-circuits to the abort path when such text arrives. ([`src/gateway/chat-abort.ts:13`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/src/gateway/chat-abort.ts#L13), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+- **Docs and changelog record shipped interrupt behavior:** The public docs say `/stop` aborts the current run and `sessions.steer` is the interrupt-and-steer variant; the changelog records both `/stop` aborting between tool calls and Telegram keeping true abort requests on the control lane. ([`CHANGELOG.md:4702`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c/CHANGELOG.md#L4702), [a2221d6b4709](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a2221d6b47094cd34c12025ce809aba02c5e1c1c))
+
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

([`docs/plugins/community.md:9`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/plugins/community.md#L9), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **External plugin install flow is first-class:** OpenClaw documents external community plugins as a supported model and installs them via `openclaw plugins install`, with ClawHub-first resolution. ([`docs/tools/plugin.md:11`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/tools/plugin.md#L11), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Channel-specific runtime is expected to live in plugins:** The WeChat channel docs explicitly state the code does not live in the core repo, that OpenClaw provides the generic channel contract, and that the external plugin owns the channel-specific runtime while core stays channel-agnostic. ([`docs/channels/wechat.md:27`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/channels/wechat.md#L27), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **No Rocket.Chat implementation on current main:** A repo search on current main found no `rocketchat`, `Rocket.Chat`, or `rocket chat` matches under `docs`, `extensions`, `src`, `package.json`, or `pnpm-lock.yaml`. ([cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Issue context already points to an external plugin:** The issue comments say a Rocket.Chat plugin was published separately (`openclaw-rocketchat`), which matches the repo's community-plugin direction instead of a core feature addition.
+
 So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.
